message:
fix(postgres): prevent hang in PgAsyncConnection by using push() instead of feed()

receive_message_no_cx() called reader.feed() which both appends raw
bytes to the internal buffer AND parses all complete messages,
returning them in a Vec.  The caller only checked for Err, silently
discarding the Ok(messages).  When the loop continued, next_message()
at the top of the loop found an empty buffer (feed had already
consumed everything) and returned None, causing the loop to block
forever on the socket read — even though postgres had already
sent a complete auth handshake response.

The fix adds a push() method to MessageReader that only appends bytes
without parsing, then uses it in receive_message_no_cx().  This lets
next_message() at the top of the loop do all the parsing, which is
the correct design: read bytes -> append -> loop back -> parse.

Fixes #9

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
